rationale: A precise, overwhelming strike will shatter their morale and expose their false narrative of resilience, reasserting our dominance.
Forward-deploying our fleet off their coast applies coercive pressure and signals unwavering resolve to escalate if needed.
A rapid, shallow incursion seizes tactical ground and fractures their defensive cohesion, keeping them off balance.
A high-impact, deniable cyber offensive against Tethys energy infrastructure will exploit their heightened vulnerability and complement Krasnov’s kinetic pressure.
Covert disruption of key supply nodes behind enemy lines will degrade operational tempo and reinforce the perception of systemic instability.
Orchestrating a provocation under false pretenses will discredit their information campaign and fracture international support.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to offset sanction-driven deficits and fund critical military-industrial expansion.
Devaluing our currency will undermine Tethys’ import-dependent economy and erode the purchasing power of their sanctions-backed financial leverage.
Restricting dual-use technology exports to Novaris will exploit their supply chain dependencies and retaliate for their alignment with Tethys.
Strengthening our entrenched positions will exploit Novaris’s logistical strain and turn captured terrain into a deadly grind for their forces.
Increasing frontline vigilance will disrupt enemy reconnaissance and prevent surprise offensives while we consolidate gains.
Probing enemy movements with stealth and precision ensures we anticipate their next move and maintain tactical superiority despite resource constraints.
Our intelligence confirms infiltration risks are rising as Novaris shifts to covert operations, so safeguarding our military and political assets is critical to maintaining defensive resilience.
With Novaris-linked cyberattacks disrupting major powers, fortifying our financial and command systems will prevent destabilizing breaches during this vulnerable phase.
Providing verified insights on Novaris’s logistical weaknesses to Tethys strengthens coalition unity and enables precision targeting without direct offensive escalation.

Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to extend our financial runway beyond 11 weeks, directly supporting the military’s defensive fortification efforts without increasing external dependency.
Negotiating preferential trade terms with Tethys leverages our diplomatic momentum to secure critical inputs and stabilize supply chains under sanctions pressure.
Strategically weakening the Novaris currency through targeted forex operations exploits their economic vulnerabilities and offsets their resource advantage amid ongoing territorial losses.

Leveraging our strong international support and recent battlefield resilience, we must deepen military and political coordination with Tethys to solidify long-term commitment and deter further Novaris aggression.
Convening a formal multilateral forum under liberal institutions will institutionalize support, reinforce normative backing, and signal that our defense is a shared security imperative.

Inviting neutral international observers to verify our conduct and document violations strengthens our moral authority, counters disinformation, and reinforces the credibility of our diplomatic position.
